tip: The most basic Chinese greeting. Works everywhere, anytime.
Doubling makes it softer. Chinese loves to repeat for emphasis.
Essential for markets and shops. Follow up with '太贵了' (too expensive) for bargaining!
Point at what you want and say this. Works in any shop or restaurant.
Put the thing you're looking for BEFORE 在哪里. Chinese puts the topic first!
Chinese says 'left turn' not 'turn left'. The direction comes first — this is Chinese thinking!
Same pattern: direction first, then action. 右转 = right turn.
Useful in taxis! Combined with 左转 and 右转 you can navigate anywhere.
The magic bargaining phrase. Say it with feeling, then watch the price drop. 了 adds emotional emphasis.
听不懂 uses a result complement: listen + not + understand. Chinese describes WHAT HAPPENED, not just the action.
One of the most useful phrases. Chinese people are generally very helpful to foreigners!
好 + verb is a common pattern: 好吃 (good to eat), 好喝 (good to drink), 好看 (good to look at).
Essential in Sichuan! But be warned: even '微辣' (slightly spicy) can be intense.
In casual restaurants, just say '买单！' loudly. In nicer places, gesture to the waiter first.
Key grammar: NO 'be' verb! Don't say '我是舒服'. Chinese adjectives connect directly: 我 + 很 + 舒服.
堵 means 'blocked/stuck'. You'll hear this a lot during rush hour (高峰期) in Chinese cities!
Also called 打的 (dǎ dī). The ride-hailing app DiDi (滴滴) gets its name from this!

pinyin: kě yǐ yòng wēi xìn ma
tip: Almost everywhere in mainland China accepts WeChat Pay. This question is mostly a formality!
Literally means 'empty the glass!' At formal dinners, touch glasses below the other person's to show respect.
Add 万事如意 (wàn shì rú yì — may everything go as you wish) for extra warmth during Spring Festival.
